2605.30136.title: Enhancing Multi-Agent Communication through Attention Steering with Context Relevance
2605.30136.paper_summary: LLM-based multi-agent systems have demonstrated remarkable performance on complex tasks through collaborative reasoning. However, these systems tend to rapidly accumulate extremely long conversation histories during interaction. As conversations lengthen, relevant information is increasingly diluted by irrelevant context, leading to degraded performance. In this work, we present Agent-Radar, a training-free context management method that dynamically steers each agent's attention toward relevant context with a novel temporal and spatial decay mechanism. Our experiments demonstrate that Agent-Radar outperforms state-of-the-art methods across five different benchmarks, yielding gains of up to 7.64 absolute points. Furthermore, our analysis shows that Agent-Radar remains effective and robust as the number of agents and interaction rounds increases. Finally, the ablation study shows that core components in Agent-Radar are crucial to performance and generalizable in different settings.
2605.30136.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基於的多代理系統已在複雜任務中通過協作推理展現出卓越的性能。<lb/>然而，這些系統在互動過程中往往會迅速累積極長的對話歷史。<lb/>隨著對話的延長，相關信息越來越容易被無關的上下文稀釋，導致性能下降。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提出了 Agent-Radar，一種無需訓練的上下文管理方法，通過一種新穎的時間和空間衰減機制動態地引導每個代理的注意力朝向相關上下文。<lb/>我們的實驗表明，Agent-Radar 在五個不同的基準測試中超越了最先進的方法，獲得了高達 7.64 的絕對點數增益。<lb/>此外，我們的分析顯示，隨著代理數量和互動輪次的增加，Agent-Radar 仍然保持有效和穩健。<lb/>最後，消融研究顯示，Agent-Radar 中的核心組件對性能至關重要，並且在不同環境中具有可泛化性。
2605.30136.author: Hongxiang Zhang et.al.
2605.30136.authors: Hongxiang Zhang, Yuan Tian, Tianyi Zhang
